page: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor architecture (x86_64) operating at 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local ephemeral storage or onboard GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64ls_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, achieving top-tier status in Geekbench multi-core testing and image processing. Its memory subsystem shows high read and write bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads reaching 258.84 GB/sec. The processor's architectural extensions contribute to strong performance in cryptographic operations and database tasks, as evidenced by top-tier Passmark database operations and strong Redis SET throughput. For artificial intelligence workloads, the hardware delivers strong LLM inference speeds for small and medium models, though large-model text generation falls into the average performance tier.

The primary tradeoff of the D64ls_v6 is its low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU and the complete absence of local storage, which requires external premium storage attachment for persistent data. This resource allocation makes the instance cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, optimizing the hardware spend per CPU cycle. It is appropriate for multi-threaded compilation, static web serving, image processing, and database operations that fit within the 128 GB memory boundary.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance relies on external premium storage. It delivers top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, image processing, and database operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth and LLM inference capabilities for small to medium models. The 2.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio represents a clear tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute density over memory capacity. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-heavy tasks, making it appropriate for multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and compute-bound database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v6 is a compute-optimized instance with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v6 is a compute-optimized instance with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A compute-dense virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and optimized memory allocation for CPU-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, image processing, and database operations
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector math
Lacks local ephemeral storage, requiring external premium storage volumes
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and compute-heavy workloads
